name: New York SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Provides electronic benefits that can be used like cash to purchase food for low-income individuals and families. Apply online at myBenefits.ny.gov, by mail, or in person at your local Department of Social Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working people, senior citizens, the disabled, and others who need help feeding their families. Must meet income and resource limits.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by household size. ABAWDs must meet work requirements as of March 2026.
hours: Contact local DSS office for hours
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via myBenefits portal

name: myBenefits NY
description: New York State's online portal for screening eligibility and applying for SNAP, HEAP, and other public assistance programs. Available 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Any New York State resident can use the screening tool to check eligibility for multiple benefit programs.
languages: English, Spanish, Arabic, Bengali, Chinese, French, Haitian-Creole, Italian, Korean, Polish, Russian, Urdu, Yiddish

name: NY State of Health Marketplace
description: New York's official health plan marketplace where individuals and families can apply for Medicaid, the Essential Plan, Child Health Plus, and Qualified Health Plans.
eligibilitySummary: All New York residents can apply. Medicaid and Essential Plan have year-round enrollment; Qualified Health Plans have annual open enrollment periods.
incomeLimitNotes: Medicaid: up to 138% FPL. Essential Plan: up to 200% FPL (incomes below ~$30,000 for individuals). QHP subsidies available at higher incomes.

name: New York State Medicaid
description: Provides free or low-cost health coverage to eligible low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly adults, and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled persons who are New York residents.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally up to 138% of the Federal Poverty Level for adults; higher limits for children and pregnant women.

name: Essential Plan
description: A New York State health insurance plan for low-income adults who do not qualify for Medicaid. Covers doctor visits, prescriptions, hospital care, and more with no monthly premium for most enrollees.
eligibilitySummary: New York residents ages 19-64 with incomes up to 200% FPL who are not eligible for Medicaid or employer coverage.
incomeLimitNotes: Income up to 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (approximately $30,120/year for an individual in 2025). No monthly premium for most enrollees.

name: Child Health Plus (CHP)
description: Free or low-cost health insurance for children under 19 who are not eligible for Medicaid and do not have other health insurance coverage.
eligibilitySummary: Children under age 19 who are NYS residents, not eligible for Medicaid, and do not have other health insurance.
incomeLimitNotes: No premium for families below 2.2x FPL. Sliding scale premiums of $15-$60/child/month for families up to 400% FPL. No co-payments for services.

name: NYS Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary weekly payments to eligible workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own, earned sufficient wages during a base period, and are able and available to work.
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned at least $3,400 in the highest-paid quarter of the base period. Weekly benefit amount is based on prior earnings.
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: NYS Career Centers
description: One-stop career centers throughout New York State providing job search assistance, resume writing, interview preparation, skills assessments, training referrals, and virtual career center services.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all New York State residents seeking employment, career development, or job training services.
hours: Varies by location; virtual services also available

name: NYS DOL Veterans Employment Services
description: Specialized employment services for veterans including job search assistance, career counseling, and connections to veteran-specific employment opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, transitioning service members, and eligible spouses seeking employment assistance in New York State.

name: NYS Homes and Community Renewal (HCR)
description: New York State's housing agency that administers affordable housing programs, Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, and other rental assistance programs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Low- to moderate-income New York residents seeking affordable housing assistance. Eligibility varies by program.
incomeLimitNotes: Section 8: Generally at or below 50% of Area Median Income. Other programs vary by location and household size.

name: HCR Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program
description: Helps low-income New Yorkers rent or buy safe, affordable housing by paying part of the rent directly to the landlord each month. The voucher stays with the household as long as they remain eligible.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 50% of Area Median Income. Priority given to extremely low-income families (below 30% AMI).
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county and household size. Generally at or below 50% of Area Median Income.

name: HCR Find Affordable Housing
description: Online tool to search for affordable housing developments across New York State, including HCR-financed properties with below-market rents.
eligibilitySummary: Low- to moderate-income New York residents seeking affordable rental housing.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by property and program.

name: Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
description: Helps income-eligible New Yorkers pay the cost of heating their homes. Provides regular heating benefits, emergency heating assistance, and cooling assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Income-eligible New York State residents who need help paying heating costs. Benefit amount depends on heating source.
incomeLimitNotes: A family of four can have a maximum gross monthly income of $6,680 (annual gross income of $80,165). Benefits up to $996 per eligible household.
hours: Regular HEAP: Dec 1 - Apr 7 or until funds exhausted. Emergency HEAP opens Jan 2.

name: NYS Temporary Assistance (Cash Assistance)
description: Provides temporary cash assistance to needy adults and children through Family Assistance (FA) and Safety Net Assistance (SNA) programs for those who cannot work, cannot find a job, or whose job does not pay enough.
eligibilitySummary: Needy individuals and families who are unable to work, cannot find a job, or whose income is insufficient. Must meet income and resource limits.
incomeLimitNotes: Family Assistance: limited to 60 months lifetime. Safety Net Assistance available for those who exhaust FA or do not qualify.
hours: Contact local DSS office for hours

name: Lifeline Telephone/Internet Discount Program
description: Provides a discount on monthly telephone or internet service to help income-eligible consumers afford communications services. Discount of $5.25 for phone only or $9.25 for internet service.
eligibilitySummary: New York residents with income at or below 135% of Federal Poverty Guidelines, or participants in qualifying assistance programs such as Medicaid, SNAP, or SSI.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL, or participation in a qualifying assistance program. One discount per household.

name: 211 New York State
description: Free, confidential information and referral service connecting New Yorkers to local health and human services resources for food, housing, utilities, elder care, health, mental health, substance use, and disaster assistance. Available 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all New York State residents. Free and confidential.
languages: English, Spanish, and 170+ languages via interpreter services

name: The Legal Aid Society
description: The nation's oldest and largest not-for-profit legal services organization, providing free legal representation to low-income New Yorkers in criminal, civil, and juvenile rights matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of New York City facing criminal charges, civil legal issues (housing, immigration, family law), or juvenile justice matters.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines; varies by program.

name: Legal Services NYC
description: Provides free legal help to low-income New Yorkers across all five boroughs in areas including housing, public benefits, immigration, family law, and consumer protection.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of New York City facing civil legal issues.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines.

name: New York Legal Assistance Group (NYLAG)
description: Civil legal services organization combating economic, racial, and social injustice by providing free legal assistance to people experiencing poverty or in crisis in areas including housing, healthcare, immigration, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in the New York City area facing civil legal problems.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines.

name: Legal Aid Society of Northeastern New York (LASNNY)
description: Provides free civil legal aid to low-income residents in northeastern New York, covering issues including housing, public benefits, family law, and consumer problems.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of northeastern New York counties needing civil legal assistance.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines based on federal poverty level.

name: Legal Aid Society of Mid-New York (LASMNY)
description: Provides free legal services to low-income residents across central New York, covering housing, family, public benefits, and consumer law issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of central New York needing civil legal assistance.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines.

name: LawNY (Legal Assistance of Western New York)
description: Non-profit law firm providing free civil legal assistance to low-income individuals in western New York on issues including housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of western New York counties needing civil legal assistance.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines.

name: LawHelpNY
description: Online portal to find free legal help anywhere in New York State. Search by county and legal issue to find local legal aid organizations and self-help resources.
eligibilitySummary: Any New York resident can use the portal to find local free legal services and resources.

name: Legal Services of Central New York
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents across the central New York region, defending dignity, strengthening communities, and securing justice.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the central New York region needing civil legal assistance.
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet income eligibility guidelines.

name: WIC Program (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Free nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and referrals for pregnant women, new mothers, and children under age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant women, postpartum and breastfeeding mothers, infants, and children under age 5 who meet income guidelines. Auto-qualify if on SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, or Essential Plan.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level. Participants in SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, Essential Plan, or Head Start may automatically qualify.
hours: Contact local WIC office for hours

name: Hunger Solutions New York
description: Statewide anti-hunger organization that helps New Yorkers access SNAP, WIC, school meals, and other nutrition assistance programs. Provides a SNAP Helpline and eligibility screening tool.
eligibilitySummary: Any New York resident seeking help with food assistance programs. Provides SNAP application assistance and eligibility screening.

name: Food Bank For New York City
description: Feeding America member food bank providing food and resources to more than 1.5 million New Yorkers through a network of over 800 community-based organizations across the five boroughs.
eligibilitySummary: Any New York City resident in need of food assistance. No income verification required at most distribution sites.

name: Regional Food Bank of Northeastern New York
description: Feeding America member food bank serving 23 counties in northeastern New York through a network of over 1,000 charitable agencies including food pantries, soup kitchens, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of northeastern New York counties in need of food assistance.

name: FeedMore Western New York
description: Feeding America member food bank procuring and distributing nutritious food and support throughout Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, and Niagara counties in western New York.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, and Niagara counties in need of food assistance.

name: Foodlink (Rochester)
description: Feeding America member food bank serving the Greater Rochester and Finger Lakes region across 10 counties. Operates mobile markets, community cafe, and culinary training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Allegany, Genesee, Livingston, Monroe, Ontario, Orleans, Seneca, Wayne, Wyoming, and Yates counties in need of food assistance.

name: Food Bank of the Southern Tier
description: Feeding America member food bank serving Broome, Chemung, Schuyler, Steuben, Tioga, and Tompkins counties through more than 300 food pantries, meal programs, shelters, and senior programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Broome, Chemung, Schuyler, Steuben, Tioga, and Tompkins counties in need of food assistance.

name: Long Island Cares
description: Feeding America member food bank serving Long Island communities through food distribution, nutrition education, and community support programs.
eligibilitySummary: Long Island residents in need of food assistance.

name: Feeding New York State
description: Statewide association of food banks representing all Feeding America member food banks in New York, coordinating hunger relief efforts across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Connects New Yorkers to their regional food bank for food assistance.

name: NYS Department of Veterans' Services (DVS)
description: Advocates for veterans, service members, and their families. Provides benefits advising, the Gold Star Annuity Program, Blind Annuity Program, veterans education approvals, and connections to federal VA benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active service members, and their families who are New York State residents.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by specific program. Gold Star and Blind Annuity programs have specific eligibility criteria.

name: NYS Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Helps low-income families pay for child care so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training. Administered by local social services districts and overseen by OCFS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children who need child care to work, attend school, or participate in training. Must meet income and program requirements.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county and family size. Families receiving Temporary Assistance may automatically qualify.
hours: Contact local social services district for hours

name: NYC Human Resources Administration (HRA)
description: New York City's social services agency fighting poverty and income inequality. Administers SNAP, Cash Assistance, Medicaid, rental assistance vouchers, and other essential benefit programs.
eligibilitySummary: New York City residents in need of public assistance, food benefits, health insurance, or other social services.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program.
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, Arabic, Bengali, Haitian Creole, Korean, and other languages

name: ACCESS HRA
description: NYC's online portal and mobile app for applying for and managing benefit programs including SNAP, Cash Assistance, Fair Fares NYC, and health insurance. View case information, submit documents, and recertify benefits online.
eligibilitySummary: New York City residents applying for or managing HRA benefit programs.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program.
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, Arabic, Bengali, Haitian Creole, Korean

name: ACCESS NYC
description: Online benefits screening tool that helps New York City residents determine eligibility for over 30 city, state, and federal benefit programs regardless of immigration status. Apply directly for SNAP, Cash Assistance, and Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: All New York City residents can screen for benefits regardless of immigration status, even if already receiving benefits or employed.
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, Arabic, Bengali, Haitian Creole, Korean, Polish, Urdu

name: NYC 311
description: New York City's main hub for government information and non-emergency services. Provides access to over 2,000 city services and programs via phone, web, text, and mobile app.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all New York City residents and visitors for non-emergency city services and information.
languages: English, Spanish, and 175+ languages via interpreter services

name: Fair Fares NYC
description: City program providing a 50% discount on subway and eligible bus fares (including Staten Island Railway, Roosevelt Island Tram, and Hudson Rail Link) for low-income New York City residents.
eligibilitySummary: New York City residents with household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must reside in one of the five boroughs.
incomeLimitNotes: 150% FPL: approximately $23,000 for an individual, $48,000 for a family of four.
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, Arabic, Bengali, Haitian Creole, Korean

name: MTA Reduced-Fare Program
description: Provides half-price fares on New York City subways, buses, and commuter railroads for seniors 65+ and people with qualifying disabilities. Reduced fare is $1.50 for subway/local bus.
address: Apply by mail: MTA Reduced Fare Program, 130 Livingston St., Brooklyn, NY 11201-9625
eligibilitySummary: Seniors aged 65 and older, or people with qualifying disabilities. Must apply in person at a Customer Service Center or by mail.
incomeLimitNotes: No income requirement. Based on age (65+) or documented disability.
hours: Customer Service Centers open 24/7 at select subway stations

name: NYC HRA Energy Assistance
description: Helps New York City residents apply for HEAP and other energy assistance programs to pay heating and cooling costs. Apply online through ACCESS HRA.
eligibilitySummary: Income-eligible New York City residents who need help paying heating or cooling costs.
incomeLimitNotes: Same as HEAP state income guidelines. A family of four: max gross monthly income of $6,680.
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, Arabic, Bengali, Haitian Creole, Korean

name: NYC HRA Career Services
description: Helps Cash Assistance clients build skills and experience needed to achieve career success and financial stability, connecting individuals to employment opportunities matching their skills, interests, and goals.
eligibilitySummary: New York City Cash Assistance recipients seeking employment, career development, or job training.
incomeLimitNotes: Must be a current Cash Assistance recipient.

name: NYS OTDA Programs and Services
description: Central hub for all programs administered by the NYS Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance, including SNAP, HEAP, Temporary Assistance, child support, and disability determinations.
eligibilitySummary: New York State residents seeking various public assistance programs.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program.
